title: Explore the power of plant medicine
description: KNYSNA NEWS - A unique and enriching event...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - A unique and enriching event exploring the healing power of plant medicine will be held on Saturday 23 August at The Node, Oaks on Main.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - A unique and enriching event exploring the healing power of plant medicine will be held on Saturday 23 August at The Node, Oaks on Main."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Hosted by Carlo Randall, this free of charge session invites the public to engage with the deep-rooted traditions of indigenous healing, paired with insights from modern science."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Titled \"Where Science Meets Tradition\", the event will feature live storytelling focused on indigenous medicinal flora, as well as cultural demonstrations including smudging rituals, herbal teas, salves, and more.","html":"Titled \"Where Science Meets Tradition\", the event will feature live storytelling focused on indigenous medicinal flora, as well as cultural demonstrations including smudging rituals, herbal teas, salves, and more."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"It's about understanding healing through both indigenous and Western lenses,\" explains Randall.","html":"\"It's about understanding healing through both indigenous and Western lenses,\" explains Randall."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Participants will gain an understanding of how plant-based healing practices are used by indigenous communities - and how these traditions align with and differ from Western medical approaches.","html":"Participants will gain an understanding of how plant-based healing practices are used by indigenous communities - and how these traditions align with and differ from Western medical approaches."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"An open Q&A session will allow attendees to engage directly with the topic, ask questions, and learn more about the significance of ancestral plant knowledge in contemporary health and sustainability efforts.","html":"An open Q&A session will allow attendees to engage directly with the topic, ask questions, and learn more about the significance of ancestral plant knowledge in contemporary health and sustainability efforts."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Why does it matter?","html":"Why does it matter?"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The event aims to highlight the vital connection between land, health and heritage. It sheds light on how indigenous communities continue to preserve plant wisdom, offering valuable lessons in resilience, care for the Earth and collective healing.","html":"The event aims to highlight the vital connection between land, health and heritage. It sheds light on how indigenous communities continue to preserve plant wisdom, offering valuable lessons in resilience, care for the Earth and collective healing."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The session is open to all ages. Donations are welcome and will go toward supporting future educational and cultural events."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For more information, contact Carlo Randall on 081 406 5694 or email [email protected]","html":"For more information, contact Carlo Randall on 081 406 5694 or email [email protected]"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Explore the power of plant medicine
